clause-variation.deleted-text: Barriers and fences for swimming pools are regulated by the Building Act 1975 (QLD), Building Regulation 2021 (QLD) and the Queensland Development Code MP 3.4 - Swimming pool barriers.
Barriers and fences for swimming pools are regulated by the Northern Territory of Australia Swimming Pool Safety Act 2004.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A swimming pool with a depth of water more than 300 mm and which is associated with a Class 2 or 3 building or Class 4 part of a building, must have suitable barriers to restrict access by young children to the immediate pool surrounds in accordance with—

ol.li: AS 1926.1, except ‘This clause shall not apply to boundary barriers.’ in clause 2.3.1 is replaced with ‘This clause shall apply to boundary barriers.’; and

subclause.title: WA REPLACE SubClause
subclause.p: A swimming pool with a depth of water more than 300 mm and which is associated with a Class 2 or 3 building or Class 4 part of a building, must have suitable barriers to restrict access by young children to the immediate pool surrounds in accordance with—

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A water recirculation system in a swimming pool with a depth of water more than 300 mm must comply with AS 1926.3.

subclause.title: SA INSERT SubClause
subclause.p: For the purposes of clause 6.1.1 of AS 1926.3, a skimmer box is an outlet, and must have a means of releasing the vacuum pressure should the suction become blocked.

subclause.title: SA INSERT SubClause
subclause.p: A first aid and cardiopulmonary resuscitation sign must—

ol.li: be attached to the safety barrier of the swimming pool, or displayed near the swimming pool; and
be at least 300 mm by 300 mm in size; and
be made of durable and weatherproof material; and
show information about the procedures for providing first aid, including performing cardiopulmonary resuscitation.

callout.title: Cross-volume considerations
callout.p: Part C2 of NCC Volume Three sets out the requirements for pumped discharge from swimming pools.
